provenance: Snapshot of culturenet-brain docs/research/parish-watch/alerts.jsonl (Parish Watch baseline sweep, PR #388). Refreshed by PR on the sweep cadence: red weekly, amber biweekly, full sweep monthly. Wording here is the site's own, written under the editorial guardrails; every item cites its sources.

alerts.whatChanged: The last Lithuanian Catholic parish in metro Detroit is under evaluation in the Archdiocese of Detroit's Families of Parishes restructuring. No closure decree has been issued. The Archdiocese's Listening Session Feedback Survey — the public window to shape the outcome — closes July 31, 2026.

sources.title: Southfield's Divine Providence Lithuanian church seeks to remain a standalone ethnic parish

alerts.whatChanged: Closed September 30, 2023 under the Archdiocese of Hartford's restructuring plan. Parishioners retained a canon lawyer and continue to contest the closure; the community has so far prevented a complete ending and the canonical fight remains active.

sources.title: Parishioners fight Waterbury church closure
Save St. Joseph's Lithuanian Catholic Church

alerts.whatChanged: Closed January 5, 2026 — the final Mass was celebrated, the bells tolled, and the lights went out after more than a century. The diocese cited roughly $250,000 in needed exterior repairs and about $20,000 in annual operating costs; regular Masses had already ended in 2021 after the 2015 merger into St. Charles Borromeo Parish (Ashland).

sources.title: Lights go out on longtime Girardville church
Saint Vincent de Paul Church in Girardville set to close

alerts.whatChanged: Open on a reduced schedule while its long-term future remains undecided. In September 2025 the Diocese of Brooklyn confirmed the church is not closing: weekday Masses moved to St. Stanislaus Kostka, weekend Masses continue at Transfiguration. A petition of more than 700 signatures, a Knights of Lithuania landmark-designation push, and an October 2025 visit from Lithuanian President Gitanas Nausėda have kept the case in the spotlight. Its fate is canonically unresolved.

sources.title: Transfiguration Church not closing, diocese says
Lithuanian Catholic community rallies for Transfiguration
President Nausėda visits Transfiguration

alerts.entity: Our Lady of the Immaculate Conception
alerts.whatChanged: Consolidated into St. Patrick Parish (White Haven) effective July 1, 2026, under the Diocese of Scranton's Vision 2030 pastoral plan.
alerts.caveat: The research record holds a single-source entry for an early mixed Lithuanian–Polish–Slovak–Rusyn parish in Freeland. Whether Our Lady of the Immaculate Conception is that parish's continuation is not yet confirmed — the identity check is an open research task.

sources.title: Three new parish consolidations to take effect July 1

alerts.whatChanged: The vacant former Lithuanian church at 15–21 East 23rd Street went before Bayonne's Planning Board in August 2025 with a proposal to demolish it for a six-story, 114-unit residential building; the city's historical committee raised no objection.

sources.title: Development plans for 15–21 East 23rd Street

alerts.whatChanged: The parish relocated to St. Lucy Church in September 2025 and continues there; its original 1942 building at 1703 Jackson Street has been on the commercial market since spring 2026, listed at $350,000.

sources.title: Saint Michael Parish finds new home at historic Saint Lucy Church
1703 Jackson Street commercial listing

alerts.entity: Sts. Peter and Paul (Šv. Petro ir Povilo)
alerts.whatChanged: Current archdiocesan planning documents (Planning Area K) list it within the combined "St. Adalbert and Sts. Peter and Paul" parish, with one Mass remaining at the Lithuanian church. No decree on its future has surfaced; its fate is canonically unresolved.

sources.title: Planning Area K — Eastern Union

alerts.whatChanged: Parishioners were formally notified of a proposed partial closure — part of an archdiocese-wide restructuring of 212 parishes toward 127 — with parishioners pushing for a Lithuanian cultural center in the building.

sources.title: Hartford parishioners met with notice of partial closure for Holy Trinity

alerts.entity: St. Ann of the Dunes (Šv. Onos)
alerts.whatChanged: Named in the Diocese of Gary's Porter Planning Area, which is consolidating five parishes down to two worship sites under the diocese's March 2026 pastoral-planning announcement.

sources.title: Pastoral planning

campaigns.who: The parish community, with this project's public analysis
campaigns.form: The parish's case made in the Archdiocese's own published numbers — The Hearth's workbook analysis and the armory on this site — while the restructuring survey window is open
campaigns.state: Press coverage describes the parish as seeking to remain a standalone ethnic parish inside the Archdiocese of Detroit's 2025–2027 restructuring. No proposal naming the parish has surfaced; the survey closes July 31, 2026.

sources.title: Southfield's Divine Providence Lithuanian church seeks to remain a standalone ethnic parish

campaigns.who: Parishioners of Holy Trinity, Hartford, and the broader Lithuanian Catholic community
campaigns.form: GoFundMe to retain a canon-law attorney; community advocacy and documentation; push to preserve the building as a Lithuanian cultural center
campaigns.state: Parishioners were formally notified of a proposed partial closure as part of the Archdiocese of Hartford's restructuring of 212 parishes. The canonical fund is active and the case is being contested.

sources.title: Hartford parishioners met with notice of partial closure for Holy Trinity
Protect Holy Trinity Church

campaigns.who: Parishioners of St. Joseph's Lithuanian Catholic Church, Waterbury
campaigns.form: Retained a canon lawyer in 2023 to contest the archdiocese's closure decision; GoFundMe to cover legal expenses
campaigns.state: Closed September 30, 2023 by decree under the Archdiocese of Hartford's restructuring plan. The canonical appeal is ongoing and the community has so far prevented a complete ending.

sources.title: Parishioners fight Waterbury church closure
Save St. Joseph's Lithuanian Catholic Church

campaigns.who: Parishioners and preservation supporters, backed by the Knights of Lithuania
campaigns.form: A petition of more than 700 signatures, a landmark-designation effort for the church building, and an October 2025 visit from Lithuanian President Gitanas Nausėda
campaigns.state: The Diocese of Brooklyn confirmed in September 2025 that the church stays open on a limited basis — weekend Masses continue at Transfiguration — and states the church building itself is not for sale. The parish's long-term future remains canonically unresolved.

sources.title: Transfiguration church not closing
Nausėda visit

campaigns.who: Parishioners of the 1901-founded parish, through its own leadership
campaigns.form: Requests to the Franciscan Fathers in Lithuania for a second Lithuanian-speaking priest, to sustain Lithuanian-language liturgy and draw younger immigrant leadership
campaigns.state: The parish remains active within the South Boston Seaport Catholic Collaborative, with Sunday Lithuanian Mass continuing; no indication yet that the request has been filled.

sources.title: St. Peter Lithuanian parish in Boston: new challenges
